**Inver Grove Heights** is a city in [Dakota County](/wiki/Dakota_County,_Minnesota "Dakota County, Minnesota"), [Minnesota](/wiki/Minnesota "Minnesota"), United States. The population was 35,801 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[3]](#cite_note-2020_Census_(City)-3) The city was formed on March 9, 1965, with the merger of the village of Inver Grove and Inver Grove Township.[[5]](#cite_note-About-5)

It is one of 186 cities and townships in the seven-county [Minneapolis-Saint Paul](/wiki/Minneapolis-Saint_Paul "Minneapolis-Saint Paul") metropolitan area.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Inver Grove Heights is 9 miles (14 km) southeast of [Saint Paul](/wiki/Saint_Paul,_Minnesota "Saint Paul, Minnesota"); nearby communities are [South St. Paul](/wiki/South_St._Paul,_Minnesota "South St. Paul, Minnesota"), [West St. Paul](/wiki/West_St._Paul,_Minnesota "West St. Paul, Minnesota"), [Sunfish Lake](/wiki/Sunfish_Lake,_Minnesota "Sunfish Lake, Minnesota"), [Eagan](/wiki/Eagan,_Minnesota "Eagan, Minnesota"), [Newport](/wiki/Newport,_Minnesota "Newport, Minnesota"), and [Saint Paul Park](/wiki/Saint_Paul_Park,_Minnesota "Saint Paul Park, Minnesota") (the last two across the Mississippi River).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Inver_Grove_Heights,_Minnesota&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e6/Map_of_Inver_Grove_from_the_1874_Minnesota_State_Atlas_%28cropped%29.jpg/330px-Map_of_Inver_Grove_from_the_1874_Minnesota_State_Atlas_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Map_of_Inver_Grove_from_the_1874_Minnesota_State_Atlas_(cropped).jpg)

Map of Inver Grove in 1874

After the signing of the [Treaty of Traverse des Sioux](/wiki/Treaty_of_Traverse_des_Sioux "Treaty of Traverse des Sioux") in 1851, settlers from [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland") and [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany") quickly staked claims in the rolling countryside west of the [Mississippi River](/wiki/Mississippi_River "Mississippi River"). Those of Irish descent farmed the eastern part of the community, while Germans cleared the wooded land to the west for their farms. [French](/wiki/French_people "French people") and [English](/wiki/English_people "English people") settlers built their homes along the river. The township of Inver Grove Heights was named after the Irish fishing village of [Inver](/wiki/Inver,_County_Donegal "Inver, County Donegal") and the German town of [Grove](/wiki/Grove,_Germany "Grove, Germany").[[5]](#cite_note-About-5) Records show that 240 farms, four churches, and four school districts were founded by 1880.[[7]](#cite_note-7) On March 9, 1965, the village of Inver Grove and Inver Grove Township merged to form Inver Grove Heights.[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)

Historic sites in Inver Grove Heights include:

* Chief Medicine Bottle's Village: formed in 1836, became Pine Bend village and post office[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* Mendota-Wabasha Road: a military road laid out by John S. Potter in 1853. Rich Valley Road now follows the old military road.[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* District 7 School: Dakota County's first public school, established in 1854[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* Salem United Methodist Church, organized in 1854[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* Old Emanuel Lutheran Church, site of first log church in 1854; services and church school were in German[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* Church of St. Patrick: [Catholic church](/wiki/Catholic_church "Catholic church") parish established on January 9, 1856[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* Salem [Evangelical Church](/wiki/Evangelical_Synod_of_North_America "Evangelical Synod of North America"): First congregation in Minnesota of the Evangelical Association of North America was organized here on March 2, 1857[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* Josiah Burwell's House: where Inver Grove Township was first organized in 1858, at Rich Valley Road and 102nd Street[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* [Reuben Freeman House](/wiki/Reuben_Freeman_House "Reuben Freeman House") of eight gables: built in 1876 to one-up the [House of Seven Gables](/wiki/House_of_the_Seven_Gables "House of the Seven Gables")[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* Inver Grove Town Hall: first town hall building, built in 1878[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* Inver Grove Village railway depot: built in 1886[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* [Rock Island Swing Bridge](/wiki/Rock_Island_Swing_Bridge "Rock Island Swing Bridge"): Built in 1894, the double-decker [swing bridge](/wiki/Swing_bridge "Swing bridge") spanned the [Mississippi River](/wiki/Mississippi_River "Mississippi River") between Inver Grove Heights and St. Paul Park. Closed to rail traffic in 1980 and to road traffic in 1999, it opened as Swing Bridge park in 2011.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* Duke's Restaurant: built in 1906 as Pierkarski's butcher shop; restaurant offered food to railway workers; later became Jersey's Bar[[8]](#cite_note-Map-8)
* [B-52](/wiki/Boeing_B-52_Stratofortress "Boeing B-52 Stratofortress") Crash Site: on September 16, 1958, a U.S. Air force B-52D bomber crashed while on a Cold War training mission originating from Loring Air Force Base, Limestone, Maine. Seven crewmen gave their lives for their country; marker on Broderick near West Loop Court[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Inver_Grove_Heights,_Minnesota&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the city has an area of 30.12 square miles (78.01 km2), of which 27.76 square miles (71.90 km2) is land and 2.36 square miles (6.11 km2) is water.[[14]](#cite_note-Gazetteer_files-14)

Interstate Highway [494](/wiki/Interstate_494 "Interstate 494") (a [beltway](/wiki/Beltway "Beltway") bypass of Minneapolis–Saint Paul), U.S. Highway [52](/wiki/U.S._Route_52_in_Minnesota "U.S. Route 52 in Minnesota"), Minnesota Highway [55](/wiki/Minnesota_State_Highway_55 "Minnesota State Highway 55"), and Minnesota Highway [3](/wiki/Minnesota_State_Highway_3 "Minnesota State Highway 3") are four of Inver Grove Heights's main routes. 80th Street East (numbered from downtown [Saint Paul](/wiki/Saint_Paul,_Minnesota "Saint Paul, Minnesota")) crosses the city in an east–west direction.

In 2022, a meteoric impact crater was identified in the Pine Bend Bluffs Scientific and Natural Area in southern Inver Grove Heights. It has been informally named the "Pine Bend impact" and has been dated to 500 million years ago. It has a diameter of about four kilometers.[[15]](#cite_note-CBS_Minnesota-15)

### 2010 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Inver_Grove_Heights,_Minnesota&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2010 census")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census") of 2010, there were 33,880 people, 13,476 households, and 9,036 families living in the city. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 1,220.5 inhabitants per square mile (471.2/km2). There were 14,062 housing units at an average density of 506.6 per square mile (195.6/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 85.7% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 3.8% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.4% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 3.4% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.1% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Race_(U.S._Census) "Race (U.S. Census)"), 3.5% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(U.S._Census) "Race (U.S. Census)"), and 3.1% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 8.9% of the population.

There were 13,476 households, of which 32.9% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 51.4% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 11.4% had a female householder with no husband present, 4.3% had a male householder with no wife present, and 32.9% were non-families. 26.2% of all households were made up of individuals, and 8.8% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.50 and the average family size was 3.04.

The median age in the city was 38.4 years. 24.5% of residents were under the age of 18; 8.5% were between the ages of 18 and 24; 26.3% were from 25 to 44; 29.1% were from 45 to 64; and 11.8% were 65 years of age or older. The gender makeup of the city was 48.0% male and 52.0% female.

### 2000 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Inver_Grove_Heights,_Minnesota&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2000 census")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census") of 2000, there were 29,751 people, 11,257 households, and 7,924 families living in the city. The population density was 1,038.8 inhabitants per square mile (401.1/km2). There were 11,457 housing units at an average density of 400.1 per square mile (154.5/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 91.80% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 2.10% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.48% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 2.01% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.02% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._Census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)"), 1.73% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 1.86% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 4.22% of the population.

There were 11,257 households, out of which 37.3% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 56.4% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 10.3% had a female householder with no husband present, and 29.6% were non-families. 21.5% of all households were made up of individuals, and 4.7% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.62 and the average family size was 3.09.

In the city, the population was spread out, with 27.3% under the age of 18, 9.2% from 18 to 24, 33.9% from 25 to 44, 21.7% from 45 to 64, and 7.8% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 34 years. For every 100 females, there were 98.2 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 94.5 males.

The median income for a household in the city was $59,090, and the median income for a family was $68,629. Males had a median income of $45,369 versus $32,080 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the city was $25,493. About 3.0% of families and 4.2% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 5.2% of those under age 18 and 4.9% of those age 65 or over.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Inver_Grove_Heights,_Minnesota&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Economy")]

In 2007, the Metropolitan Council estimated the employment in Inver Grove Heights to be 11,158.[[17]](#cite_note-17)
The city hosts the headquarters of [CHS, Inc.](/wiki/CHS,_Inc. "CHS, Inc."), an agricultural cooperative ranked 12th in the 2007 ICA Global 300 list of mutuals and cooperatives, and 145th on the Fortune 500 list of U.S. corporations.[[18]](#cite_note-f2008-18)[[19]](#cite_note-g300-19)
[Pine Bend Refinery](/wiki/Pine_Bend_Refinery "Pine Bend Refinery"), the largest oil refinery in Minnesota and fourteenth largest in the U.S., straddles the border with [Rosemount](/wiki/Rosemount,_Minnesota "Rosemount, Minnesota"). It is operated by Flint Hills Resources.

Inver Grove Heights is also the home of Gertens Greenhouses, one of the largest garden center locations in the United States.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Inver_Grove_Heights,_Minnesota&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Education")]

The Inver Grove Heights Community Schools district or Independent School District 199 operates five schools in the city:

* [Simley High School](/wiki/Simley_High_School "Simley High School")
* Inver Grove Heights Middle School[[22]](#cite_note-22)
* Hilltop Elementary School[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* Pine Bend Elementary School[[24]](#cite_note-24)
* Salem Hills Elementary School[[25]](#cite_note-25)

There is one private school for preschool through eighth grade in Inver Grove Heights: Berea Lutheran Church.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

The western part of the city is served by [Independent School District 196](/wiki/Independent_School_District_196 "Independent School District 196"), with students attending [Dakota Hills Middle School](/wiki/Dakota_Hills_Middle_School "Dakota Hills Middle School"), [Rosemount Middle School](/wiki/Rosemount_Middle_School "Rosemount Middle School"), [Eagan High School](/wiki/Eagan_High_School "Eagan High School"), and [Rosemount High School](/wiki/Rosemount_High_School "Rosemount High School").[[27]](#cite_note-27)

Inver Grove Heights is also home to [Inver Hills Community College](/wiki/Inver_Hills_Community_College "Inver Hills Community College").

## Government and politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Inver_Grove_Heights,_Minnesota&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Government and politics")]

Inver Grove Heights's municipal government provides for a council of five members, one of whom is the mayor. The mayor is Brenda Dietrich, who was elected in 2022.

The other council members are:[[28]](#cite_note-28)

* Sue Gliva
* John Murphy
* Tony Scales
* Mary T'Kach

In general, city government is nonpartisan. Candidates need not be selected or endorsed by political parties, and by state law no such endorsement appears on the ballot. The mayor is elected every two years while council members are elected every four. Terms are staggered with two council members elected one election cycle and the two others two years later. The non-mayoral seats are elected in pairs, giving voters the chance to vote for up to two candidates. If necessary, races are narrowed down during a primary election.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

As a part of Dakota County, Inver Grove Heights's northern and central precincts (1, 8-10) join with regions northward to form the Second District on the County Commission. It has been represented by former [South St. Paul](/wiki/South_St._Paul,_Minnesota "South St. Paul, Minnesota") Mayor Kathleen A. Gaylord since 2003. The southern, eastern, and far western portions of the city (precincts 2-7) are joined by regions south and west to form the Fourth District of the County Commission, which has been represented by former State Representative and former Inver Grove Heights Mayor [Joe Atkins](/wiki/Joe_Atkins "Joe Atkins") since 2017. County commissioners serve four-year terms.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

Inver Grove Heights is in [Minnesota's 2nd congressional district](/wiki/Minnesota%27s_2nd_congressional_district "Minnesota's 2nd congressional district"), represented by [Angie Craig](/wiki/Angie_Craig "Angie Craig") since 2019.

The city is in the state's 53rd legislative district, represented in the Minnesota Senate by [Matt Klein](/wiki/Matt_Klein "Matt Klein"). Most of the city is in Minnesota House district 53A, represented by [Mary Frances Clardy](/wiki/Mary_Frances_Clardy "Mary Frances Clardy"); the northeast of the city is in House district 52B, represented by [Rick Hansen](/wiki/Rick_Hansen_(politician) "Rick Hansen (politician)").

## Sports and recreation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Inver_Grove_Heights,_Minnesota&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Sports and recreation")]

Facilities include Inver Wood Golf Course and Arbor Pointe Golf Club. There is also an 18-hole disc golf course called North Valley. The city also has a public community center with a waterpark, two sheets of ice and a full gym shared with the National Guard.
